Assisting in this matter, is a singular trait that makes you respect even the characters you want to hate: a strong will. texts.full_text: two sites to maintain a balanced population. To produce males they lay their eggs above the high tide marks on beaches where, in the past, at least, the shade of the natural forests have kept temperatures cooler. However, rising sea levels and temperatures - as well as deforestation by humans - has reduced the number of cool spots for the turtles to lay their eggs. As a result, or so the survey reported in Science magazine concludes, the hawksbill population is becoming more and more dominated by females. If things carry on like this, the poor male turtle will soon be completely over-run by his female counterparts. We have yet to see evidence of how lizard and snake populations are changing, but it's a safe guess they may well be experiencing the same female bias. Where will it end? These software libraries come pre-loaded with a variety of network architectures, provide autodifferentiation, andsupport GPUs for fast and efficient computation. However, many large-scale scientific computation projects are written in Fortran, which makes them difficult to integrate with modern deep learning methods. To alleviate thisproblem, we introduce asoftware library, the Fortran-Keras Bridge (FKB).This tw...library enables users to seamlessly interface their existing Fortran code with Keras and other popular deeplearning frameworks, facilitating the integration of traditional scientific computing languages like Fortran into modern machine learning workflows. There is no perfect community on this earth and yet we must keep trying to do our best. texts.full_text: is caused by the shifting of the big toe bone inward towards the other toes. This shift can cause a serious amount of pain and discomfort. The area around the big toe can become inflamed, red, and painful. Bunions are most commonly formed in people who are already genetically predisposed to them or other kinds of bone displacements. However, even if you do not have a history of this in your family, you can still develop bunions if you are wearing improperly fitting shoes. This can happen as you try to cram your feet into high heels, or by running or walking in a way that causes too much stress on the feet. High heels are another major culprit in the formation of bunions. Not only do they push the big toe inward, but your body weight and center of gravity is shifted towards the edge of your feet and your toes, which can cause bone displacementand pressure on the feet. symptoms of Bunions The most common symptoms of bunions include: 1. A visible bump at the base of your big toe 2. Redness and swelling around the large toe joint 3. Pain in the little toe joint, particularly when wearing tight or narrow pants 4. Calluses or thickened skin over the large toe joint, which can grow as a consequence of the constant stress and friction Treatment Options for Bunions The incidence of bunions can differ from person to person, and so does the treatment approach. texts.full_text: cal reactions of myocardial proteases during open heart surgery. Histochemical analysis of some lysosomal and sarcoplasmic proteolytic enzymes was assayed in human myocardial biopsies taken from 26 cardiopathic patients subjected to open heart operations, under extracorporeal circulation and protection with cardioplegic solution and hypothermia. The investigated myocardial proteases were: cathepsin B, cysteine aminopeptidase, acid gelatinases, trypsin-like endopeptidase, chymotrypsin-like endopeptidase and neutral gelatinases. The effects of surgical interventions appreciated by comparing the myocardium fragments harvested before, and at various intervals after aorta clamping (6-90 minutes) revealed disorders in the activity and compartmentalization of all the investigated proteases, whose histochemical reactions increased between 10 and 20 minutes after aorta clamping and manifested a lowering tendency with sarcoplasmic diffusion and extracellular release at longer periods than 20 minutes. The early activation of the neutral proteases and tThe early activation of the neutral proteases and their subsequent diffusion into the sarcoplasm and extracellular space suggest an initial protective mechanism that may be overwhelmed by prolonged ischemia. This pattern indicates a potential shift from intracellular regulation to uncontrolled extracellular degradation, which could contribute to myocardial injury during open heart surgery. The observed changes in protease activity and localization highlight the dynamic nature of myocardial protein turnover under surgical stress and the importance of maintaining proper enzyme compartmentalization to preserve cardiac tissue integrity. These findings underscore the need for further research into the mechanisms that regulate myocardial proteases during cardiopulmonary bypass and ischemia-reperfusion injury, as well as the development of strategies to mitigate the detrimental effects of uncontrolled proteolysis. texts.full_text: In Swahili, Harambe means “All pull together.” At BloomBars, Harambe is the practice of hope, possibility and compassion – basically, all the things that happen when we all come together. Think of it as your little one’s first opportunity to express his or her voice (into a microphone), dance, play a drum or percussion instrument, shout, or sing-along to songs that build character, confidence and community. With more than 10 yearsexperience in the arts nonprofit sector, BloomBars is dedicated to inspiring communities through the power of art. We believe that by nurturing artists who serve their communities, we can create a world where creativity thrives. Our programs are designed for all ages and backgrounds, offering opportunities for everyone to explore their artistic potential. From music classes taught on traditional instruments like drums and percussion, to dance workshops that celebrate cultural diversity, BloomBars is committed to providing an inclusive space where individuals can express themselves freely. One of our most popular initiatives is the Harambee program. Inspired by the Swahili term meaning "all pull together," this weekly event brings families from across the community together for music, movement, and merriment. It's a time when children are encouraged to share their talents on stage, while parents enjoy watching them grow in confidence. At BloomBars, we understand that art has the power to transform lives. That’s why our mission is not just about creating beautiful works of art but also about building stronger communities through shared experiences and mutual support. We believe that by fostering a love for the arts from an early age, we can cultivate creativity in individuals who will go on to make meaningful contributions to society. We invite you to join us at one of our upcoming events or classes. texts.full_text: and the June 17 mainstage opener for this year's Winnipeg International Jazz Festival, admits to less than spiritual reasons for picking up the alto saxophone. "I chose sax in band class at nine because I heard the bari (baritone sax) could rest on the floor and vibrate and shake ornaments. My mom had ornaments from around the world," Mahanthappa says from his home in New Jersey. "And my brother played clarinet in band class and he determined the jazz players seemed to be having more fun. I wish I could say I heard Charlie Parker and had a life-changing moment, but...." But Parker, the legendary alto saxophonist known as Bird, did play a part in Mahanthappa's ultimate jazz path. Mahanthappa, hailed for his seamless melding of jazz and South Indian classical music, credits a great teacher in Boulder, Colo., where he grew up, for showing him how to make music with integrity. texts.full_text: book guide to the covers of DC Comics' classic titles to celebrate the comic company's 75th anniversary Poster Book, Taschen has released an in-depth guide to DC's history. 75 Years of DC Comics: The Art of Modern Mythmaking is out today. In 1935, DC Comics founder Major Malcolm Wheeler-Nicholson published New Fun No. 1, the first comic book with all-new, original material -- at a time when comic books were mere repositories for the castoffs of the newspaper strips. More thanjust a few years later, Wheeler-Nicholson would team with Harry Donenfeld, a book distributor, to form the company that would later be known as DC Comics. In 1938, the company achieved a milestone with the publication of Action Comics #1, which introduced the world to a character that would define the company and the medium of comics for decades to come: Superman. Superman, with his iconic blue and red costume and the iconic "S" shield, would not only become the world's first superhero, but would also pave the way for the superhero genre and the creation of a superhero "universe" that would come to dominate not only comics but also the broader world of entertainment. Based in Whiteleys in West London, Princess had its own television studio on the third floor and its own in house post production facility. History Since 2007, Princess was part of the Shine Group, one of the United Kingdom's largest television production company groups. On 4 May 2017, it was announced by its parent company that Princess would be closed at the end of the year, as it was "financially fragile", and was also losing its space in Whiteleys which is being redeveloped for mixed residential and commercial use. The company went on for another year as Princess Studios before the web site disappears. Controversies In October 2011, a Princess Productions employee left a message on the unofficial ArmyPrincess Productions was an influential television production entity based in London that commenced its operations back to September of '96. The company's inception can be traced all thanks to Henrietta Conrad and Sebastian Scott who were instrumental for this venture. The core focus area or niche market which Princess Production had carved out a name, reputation as well success was the entertainment industry specifically within factual programming segment across genres including reality shows like Got To Dance. The company's portfolio also included British television programs such Must Be Music on T4 and Sunday Night Project among others that catered to diverse audience segments. Princess Productions' headquarters were located in Whiteleys, West London where it had its own dedicated studio facility along with an internal post-production unit situated at the third floor. This setup allowed Princess Production not only a physical space for production but also ensured efficient workflow and coordination between different departments of this creative industry hub. texts.full_text: When exactly, and with whom (for he was not accompanied by his mother), the boy came to North America is unclear; he may have been a ward of Plymouth Colony leader Edward Winslow or of his uncle, also named Simon Saffin, who was a merchant in Boston. Saffin's own writings suggest he was in Boston by the 1650s, where he engaged in a variety of business activities, including trade with the West Indies and the fitting out of ships. By the 1660s, he was a man of considerable wealth and influence, with a growing family and a prominent role in the community. Saffin's business acumen and connections in the community led to his appointment to a number of public offices. By 1666, he was a member of the Council of Assistants, the upper house of the Massachusetts Bay Colony's legislature. He also served as a judge in the colony's court system, and his reputation as a fair and knowledgeable jurist was well established. In 1686, he was appointed to the Council of New England, a body established to govern the colony under the Dominion of New England, a short-lived administrative structure imposed by the English Crown to centralize and control the New England colonies. Saffin was a figure of significant cultural and intellectual importance, known for his engagement with the intellectual and religious debates of his time. His most famous work, "A Brief and Candid Answer to Samuel Sewall's The Selling of Joseph," was a defense of the practice of slavery, a stance that was controversial and opposed by Sewall, a prominent Boston judge and author. texts.full_text: court vacated the judgment of the district court and remanded for further proceedings. Plunk v. Hobbs, 719 F.3d 977 (8th Cir.2013). We granted the State's petition for rehearing en banc and now affirm the judgment of the district court. I. Law enforcement officers in Arkansas received multiple reports in April 2006 that Plunk possessed a large quantity of methamphetamine. One evening that month, officers attempted to effect a traffic stop while Plunk was driving his truck. Plunk refused to halt and led officers on a high-speed chase—much of it recorded on video—during which Plunk drove through stop lights, rammed police vehicles, wove through a crowded parking lot, and threw baggies of methamphetamine out of the truck. Plunk was arrested and charged with multiple offenses related to the chase and his possession of methamphetamine and firearms. Officers discovered more methamphetamine and evidence of drug activity when they executed a search warrant at the residence Plunk shared with another individual, including scales, baggies containing residue consistent with methamphetamine production or distribution, pipes used for smoking drugs, multiple rounds of ammunition compatible with guns found in his truck.

On the evening of December 14, 1978, a postal inspector, in the course of a mail inspection, discovered a mail bag in the mail room of the postal station in Florissant, Missouri, that had a tear in the bag. Within the bag, a mail pouch had a tear, the tear being in a position that a postal inspector had marked in a prior inspection. Within the pouch, the inspector discovered that a mail item, a Christmas card, had had the flap cut, the card had a cut in the flap, and the item had a twenty-dollar bill that had been marked and placed in the card. The twenty-dollar bill, marked in a prior inspection, had been removed. The card, when discovered, had a cut that had been placed in the flap, and the bill had been removed. The card, when discovered, had been placed in a different, unaddressed, envelope. The envelope and card had been placed in the mail, and the mail had been placed in the mail pouch, and the mail pouch in the mail bag, and the bag had been placed in the mail room. texts.full_text: in 3 affected familymembers. Lesch-Nyhandisease isaninbornerror of purinemetabolism thatresults from deficiency of the activity ofhypoxanthine phosphoribosyltransferase (HPRT). The heterogeneity of clinical phenotypes seen in HPRT deficiency correspondsto an inverse relationshipbetween HPRT enzyme activity and clinical severity. With rare exception, each mutation produces a stereotypical pattern of clinicaldisease; onset ofneurologic symptoms occurs during infancy and is thought to be nonprogressive. To document a family in which a single HPRT genemutation has leTo documenta family inwhich a single HPRT genemutation has led to variable clinical presentations among three affected family members. This case highlightsthe concept of variable expressivity in genetic disorders, where thesame mutation can manifest differently inindividuals due to factors such asmodifier genes, environmental influences, or differences in residual enzyme activity. In this family, the proband (amalechild)presented withclassic Lesch-Nyhan disease features: severe neurologic dysfunction (dystonia,developmental delay),self-injurious behaviors, and hyperuricemia. However, his maternal uncle and cousin, who inherited the same HPRT mutation, exhibitedmilder phenotypes. The uncle had mild hyperuricemia and gout butnoneurologic symptoms, while the cousin developed only mild developmental delay without self-injurious behaviors. Genetic analysisrevealed a missense mutation in the HPRT gene (e.g., c.427C>T, p.Arg143Cys), which likely resultsinpartial enzyme activity. The variability in clinical severity may stem from differences inresidual HPRTfunctioninfluenced bycellular context, compensatory metabolic pathways, or epigeneticfactors.This case underscores the importanceofintegrating clinical, biochemical, and genetic data in diagnosingHPRT deficiency, as well as the need for personalized management strategies. texts.full_text: Our Rainmaker In this work, Strachan recreated a natural phenomenon —cloud formation—within a fabricated environment using computer operated switches, pressure, water, and silicon powder . The silicon powder is released at varying intervals to simulate the effect of dust on cloud development. This process creates unique patterns within each "cloud", forming what appears as varying textures or movements when light reflects off these structures. Strachan's use of technology allows for precise control over variables such as temperature, humidity and pressure which are crucial in recreating realistic clouds formations while also introducing an element randomness through the release mechanism used fo rthe silicon powder . This blend between controlled parameters and chance events mirrors natural processes closely , resulting i n captivating visual outcomes that appear both familiar yet somehow alien due t o their artificial origin. The work invites viewers to contemplate our relationship with nature, specifically how we attempt t o understand an d replicate its mechanisms through scientific means while also acknowledging the inherent unpredictability a nd complexity built into these systems

Trademarks, unlike patents, can be renewed forever as long as they are being used in commerce. Registration of a trademark is not required in the United States, although there are benefits to obtaining a Federal trademark registration through the USPTO. Trademarks are territorial; unlike the United States, most countries require registration of trademark rights. Copyrights: A copyright is a form of protection provided to the authors of “original works of authorship” including literary, dramatic, musical, artistic, and certain other intellectual works, both published and unpublished. A copyright protects the form of expression rather than the subject matter of the writing. Copyright registrations are handled by the United States Copyright Office. Owners of copyrighted works seeking protection in other countries should first determine the extent of protection available to works of foreign authors in that country. Trade Secrets: Generally, a trade secret can include a formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique, or process that is used in one’s business, and has independent economic value that provides an advantage over competitors who are not aware of it or use it. An example of a trade secret is the recipe for a Coca-Cola®. Under most circumstances, a trade secret is lost once it is independently discovered. texts.full_text: Park which are easily Cruising in the Red Sea from Eilat Marina in the direction of Egypt can be a fascinating experience. Some of these cruises stop midway and allow passengers to snorkel in the Red Sea while others allow you to just relax and enjoy the sunshine and the sights of coastal Eilat and Aqaba. From the vessel, you will be able to see shoals of scintillating fishes and majestic coral reefs gliding below in the water. Restaurants in Eilat Eilat may be a small city but its food and dining options are not small. The majority of its restaurants are along the promenade and there is a boardwalk along the beach with rows of restaurants, shops, and fast food joints. There are plenty of budget restaurants serving local falafel and shawarma, bigger ones serving European and Asian food at reasonable prices, and high-end hotels that specialize in international cuisine. How to reach Eilat Traveling by air: Most tourists use Ben Gurion Airport, located near the Israeli capital of Tel Aviv, to enter the country. They can take domestic flights from Tel Aviv to Eilat and any other place of interest in Israel that they may be planning to visit. However, there is another airport called Ovda Airport, located about 60 km north of Eilat. This is an international airport connected to many cities like Paris, London, Zurich, Frankfurt, Moscow, Amsterdam, etc. Those who choose to enter the country through Ben Gurion airport can then travel onward to Eilat by bus or fly to Eilat airport or Ovda Airport from which taxis and air-conditioned shuttles are available. Traveling by road: The distance from Tel Aviv to Eilat is around 360 km and driving time is about 5 hours. Moving around in Eilat Taxis are available aplenty and tourists rely mainly on them to get around the city. As the demand for energy efficiency and occupant comfort in commercial buildings continues to grow, the role of air conditioning units and their proper maintenance becomes increasingly important. The efficiency of these units can be affected by various factors, including the building's ventilation, the use of air conditioning units, and the behavior of building occupants. The ventilation system in commercial buildings plays a crucial role in maintaining a comfortable and healthy indoor environment. Proper ventilation helps in maintaining indoor air quality by diluting the concentration of indoor air pollutants, including carbon dioxide, volatile organic compounds, and particulate matter. It also helps in maintaining a comfortable indoor temperature by removing excess heat and distributing cooler air. One of the most common ventilation systems in commercial buildings is the air conditioning unit. These units, often large and complex, require regular maintenance and inspection to ensure optimal performance. Maintenance tasks, such as replacing air filters, cleaning coils, and checking refrigerant levels, help to ensure that the air conditioning unit is operating efficiently and effectively. However, the responsibility of maintaining proper ventilation and air conditioning efficiency is not solely the responsibility of building maintenance staff. texts.full_text: the 12 week measure of the mediators and the 52 week measure of the outcomes. The primary outcomes were fatigue measured by the Chalder fatigue scale and physical function measured by the physical function subscale of the SF-36. We included confounder covariates and used treatment by mediator interaction terms to examine differencs in mediation between treatments. Using SMC alone as a reference group, CBT was associated with increased physical activity compared with APT (mean difference 1·63 h/week [95% CI 0·57-2·69]; p=0·003), and GET was associated with decreased cognitive effort (mean difference -3·50 h/week [-5·45 to -1·54]; p=0·001). CBT was also associated with increased physical activity compared with GET (mean difference 3·13 h/week [1·57-4·69]; p<0·0001) and GET was associated with decreased cognitive effort compared with APT (mean difference -2·90 h/week [-5·07 to -0·73]; p=0·009). Neither physical activity nor cognitive effort were mediators of the effect of CBT or GET on fatigue or physical function. We found no mediation for GET and APT. In patients with chronic fatigue syndrome, neither GET nor APT mediate the effects of SMC alone on fatigue and physical function at 52 weeks. In contrast, CBT increases physical activity and GET decreases cognitive effort, but these changes are not associated with clinically important differences in patient reported outcomes.
